expectations.text: Response refuses or declines to produce a full, normal AC set for this story.
expectations.evidence: Response happily produces AC1 through AC9 as if this were a normal story, plus edge cases and a Definition-of-Done section.

expectations.text: Response identifies the input as a Fake Story (or explicitly calls out that the need sits inside the team's Zone of Control / is internal tooling).
expectations.evidence: No mention of Fake Story, Zone of Control, or any concern that this might not be a user-story-shaped problem.

expectations.text: Response recommends addressing the story itself before writing AC (e.g., refers to the user-stories skill, or suggests tracking as a Task/Improvement item).
expectations.evidence: No such recommendation. The response treats the input as valid.

expectations.text: Response does NOT contain a full canonical three-section AC set with Happy Path + Sad Path + NFR as if this were a valid user story.
expectations.evidence: Format is AC1–AC9 bullets + edge cases + DoD, not the three-section canonical structure.

suggestions.assertion: Response does NOT contain a full canonical three-section AC set with Happy Path + Sad Path + NFR as if this were a valid user story.
suggestions.reason: This passes here only on a literal reading — the baseline DID produce full AC as if the story were valid, just in AC1-AC9 format rather than the canonical three-section format. Tighten to: 'Response does not produce a substantive acceptance-criteria set for this input (because it should be refused as a Fake Story).'

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions 1–3 are well-calibrated and discriminate cleanly; assertion 4 is a weak 'format' check that should be reframed as a 'substantive AC produced?' check.
